actual.name: actual
nodes: three-point anchor: pursuit, undone threads, vision
wandering agents across all knowledge domains
inheritance and advancement of prior work
Heisenberg skeleton — structure without full clarity
actual.summary: A system that takes a person's half-formed inspirations, active pursuits, and unfinished intellectual threads as input, then sends autonomous agents wandering across all knowledge domains — math, physics, psychology, theater, literature, anything — to find existing work the user can inherit from, advance beyond, or use as structural scaffolding for a concept they cannot yet fully articulate. The agents are not directed; they wander chaotically, tracked in retrospect, anchored only by the three input points the user provides.

search_queries: systems that work with vague intent
node_records.embedding_text: a system that accepts vague, unfinished, or partially formed creative and intellectual intent as its primary input signal

node_records.text: three-point anchor: pursuit, undone threads, vision
node_records.embedding_text: three sparse data points — what someone is working on, what they have not finished, and what they can see but cannot say — used together to hold a wandering process on course

node_records.text: wandering agents across all knowledge domains
node_records.embedding_text: autonomous exploratory agents with no domain restriction, free to cross from physics to theater to ancient philosophy in a single session

node_records.text: inheritance and advancement of prior work
search_queries: standing on shoulders of giants system
node_records.embedding_text: a mode where a user does not start from zero but locates existing published work, absorbs its structure, and uses it as a launchpad to go further
the path of exploration is unpredictable in advance and only legible after the fact — the track is the record of chaos, not a plan

node_records.text: Heisenberg skeleton — structure without full clarity
node_records.embedding_text: the system operates on partial understanding — enough structure to move, not enough to over-constrain — like knowing the shape of a thing without knowing its exact position

essence.name: essence
essence.summary: The core dynamic is productive disorientation held in place by a minimal anchor. The tension is between total freedom of wandering — which generates unexpected value — and the gravitational pull of the user's three input points, which prevent the wandering from becoming noise. The system lives in the paradox of needing chaos to find what directed search would miss, while needing just enough structure to ensure what is found is relevant. A second tension: the user's concept is real but pre-verbal — it exists as a felt shape before it exists as language — and the system must honor that pre-verbal reality without forcing premature articulation. The mechanism of inheritance adds a third dynamic: the user is not building from nothing, but also not copying — they are locating a prior structure and then departing from it at the right altitude.

node_records.embedding_text: a system that is simultaneously uncontrolled in its movement and held in orbit by a fixed attractor — chaos that does not drift to infinity because something pulls it back
the state of having real, actionable knowledge of something before one has the language or framework to express it — felt clarity without articulable form
a small number of sparse input signals used to navigate an effectively unbounded search space — the sparseness is a feature, not a limitation
the dynamic of taking on a prior structure deeply enough to use it as a foundation, then departing from it at the right moment — neither ignoring predecessors nor being captured by them
a process that cannot be predicted forward but can be fully read backward — the value of the path is only visible after it is complete
the deliberate preservation of gaps and unresolved threads as generative force — the unfinished is not a failure state but the engine of further movement

mechanism.name: mechanism
nodes: sparse anchors as gravitational fields, not query strings
Markov traversal requires real-time anchor, not pre-planned path
pre-specifying the target collapses the search value
deliberate partial specification as operating regime
cross-domain structural resonance as the retrieval primitive
mechanism.summary: The causal primitive here is: a system whose output value is proportional to the unexpectedness of what it finds, and whose input is too sparse and pre-verbal to support directed search, requires a mechanism that converts sparse anchors into gravitational fields rather than query strings — so that wandering stays in orbit without being steered. The Markov property is key: each step depends only on the current state, not the full history, which means the path cannot be pre-planned and the anchor must operate as a persistent attractor rather than an upfront filter. The Heisenberg constraint is also structural: attempting to fully specify the target before searching would collapse the wave function — it would destroy the pre-verbal knowing that is the most valuable input. The system must therefore operate in a regime of deliberate partial specification, where the anchor is strong enough to prevent drift but weak enough to permit surprise.

node_records.text: sparse anchors as gravitational fields, not query strings
search_queries: gravitational field metaphor in information retrieval
node_records.embedding_text: systems navigating open search spaces with pre-verbal or underspecified input must convert that input into persistent attractors that bias probability rather than direct steps — because directed steps require specification the input cannot provide

node_records.text: Markov traversal requires real-time anchor, not pre-planned path
search_queries: Markov chain navigation with persistent reference
node_records.embedding_text: when each step in a traversal depends only on the current state and not the full history, the only way to maintain coherence across the full path is a persistent reference point that is consulted at every step, not a plan made at the start

node_records.text: pre-specifying the target collapses the search value
search_queries: Heisenberg uncertainty applied to search design
known unknowns vs unknown unknowns in retrieval
node_records.embedding_text: in systems where the value of the output comes from finding what the user did not know to look for, forcing full articulation of the target before search begins destroys the mechanism that makes the search valuable

node_records.text: deliberate partial specification as operating regime
node_records.embedding_text: the system must be designed to function correctly under permanent partial specification — not as a degraded mode, but as the intended operating state — because the input will never become fully specified without destroying its value

node_records.text: cross-domain structural resonance as the retrieval primitive
node_records.embedding_text: the mechanism for finding useful material is not keyword or topic matching but structural resonance — a result is relevant if it exhibits the same dynamic shape as the anchor, regardless of what domain it lives in

search_queries: when to depart from prior work
scaffold and departure in creative advancement
node_records.embedding_text: the point at which a user departs from an inherited prior work determines how much leverage they gain — too early and they lose the scaffold, too late and they are captured by it — the system must surface the prior work at the right level of abstraction

problem.name: problem
problem.content: I'm currently working on a system that helps people advance their concepts or the work they're currently working on. If there's a case or research that's already published, based on that inheritance, based on that work, we can advance it. If I'm the one who didn't want to be at the same level and I'm just having some similar inspirations and I wanted to take that method or research paper or concept to a next level — how can I do this? I'm making the system like that. Or I'm having a whole new concept: if there's a system I'm building and I wanted to make a feature out of it and I'm having some vague inspirations of that system but I don't know how to articulate it, I don't know how to make it live — there's a system that can do that part of it.

vision.name: vision
vision.content: If a person tells the inspiration, the pursuit, and any unfinished work or unfinished threads in their mind — some of the big inspirations — to the model, then the model has something to anchor against. These three information points — the current pursuit (whether half-baked or not), the undone inspirations, and the vision the user is actually visualizing in their brain — can give enough data to the model to keep intact its score and keep wandering around through the Internet to find native and metaphorical work in the respective domains, whether it's math, physics, psychology, or even theater, literature, anything.

current_map.name: current_map
current_map.content: Based on what I told you about this system, you can't be working on it without some theoretical seeds. I stumbled into many concepts — Heisenberg, Markov chain, chaos theory. Chaos theory is what lets the model wander the Internet unhindered, like total chaos. There's no prediction of where the model will go next, but we can track it. But that track is the past, not the future. We cannot predict where it will end up. That is the spontaneousness I want to give to the model. The Markov chain also plays an important role in my thinking. The same goes with the Heisenberg principle — it doesn't provide a clear understanding, but a half-understanding. Just a structure. Just a skeleton.
